scope: User-visible project conversation only. Internal instructions, private reasoning, and raw tool output are excluded.

conversation.content: Asked to break down the Marketing Team Lead Management practical test PDF and design a system for campaigns, clients, channels, and responsibilities.
Requested a README.md with flow diagrams so the full system is understood and ready to implement.
Clarified that the app should not manage campaign leads generally, but should manage leads assigned to a campaign.
Asked to focus only on lead management and create READMELeadmanagement.md with a flow diagram.
Created READMELeadmanagement.md describing a focused lead management system, data model, lifecycle, API endpoints, flow diagrams, validation rules, and implementation order.
Asked to implement the READMELeadmanagement.md plan concisely and defer deeper security concerns.
Implemented the Django app foundation with models, serializers, API routes, dashboard template, and lead CRUD behavior.
Asked to add dummy data.
Added seeded/demo records for the dashboard.
Asked whether the system supports adding, viewing, updating, deleting marketing team leads, tracking assigned campaign responsibility, and tracking progress/status.
Reviewed the implemented system against the checklist and identified that the project direction needed to support marketing team leads and assigned work, not only prospect leads.
Asked to redesign the system and update READMELeadmanagement.md.
Redesigned the documentation around TeamLead, AssignedWork, and Lead concepts.
Asked to implement the redesigned system.
Implemented team lead management and assigned work tracking with Django REST Framework APIs and a single-page dashboard.
Asked to add a manager login system and show the demo username/password on the login page.
Added Django session login protection, demo manager credentials, and protected API/dashboard access.
Asked for suggestions about fields, structure, models, UI, statuses, and categories.
Suggested a practical structure using TeamLead, AssignedWork, and Lead models with focused statuses and dashboard workflows.

Asked to build summary cards: Total Team Leads, Active Team Leads, Total Assigned Work, In Progress Work, Blocked Work, Completed Work, Average Progress, High Priority Work; also requested dark mode and icon buttons instead of text actions.
Added operational summary cards, dark mode, and compact icon-based row actions.
Asked where this type of system is used.
Explained that the system is used by marketing managers, agencies, sales/marketing operations teams, and campaign teams to track responsibilities and lead follow-up.
Reported that the View button was not working.
Fixed the View Work action so it filters assigned work by the selected team lead.
Asked to show pie charts and bar charts for more observability.
Added canvas-based pie and bar charts for work status, workload, progress, lead grade distribution, and leads per team lead.
Asked to add more data, about 20 rows.
Added around 20 demo rows for assigned work and scored lead visibility.
Asked to look at Zoho lead management features and focus on lead management.
Reviewed comparable lead management concepts and identified lead scoring, distribution, generation intake, and nurturing as useful features.
Asked to implement lead scoring based on fields, behavior, email engagement, social activity, industry, revenue, etc., and add data for scoring.
Implemented lead scoring with score inputs, automatic score/grade calculation, hot/warm/cold grades, scoring metrics, and seeded scored lead data.
Asked to make the table less messy by showing 5 rows and adding scrolling so views do not expand.
Made table areas compact and scrollable with sticky headers and clamped long text.
Asked whether lead distribution was handled.
Explained the current state and then implemented automatic lead assignment rules.

Requested auto-assignment rules: hot leads to active matching specialists, Paid Ads to Paid Ads specialists, Social Media to Social Media specialists, SEO/website to SEO specialists, fallback to lowest current lead count; also requested Unassigned Leads, Hot Unassigned Leads, and Leads Per Team Lead.
Implemented backend auto-assignment, dashboard metrics for unassigned/hot unassigned leads, and a Leads Per Team Lead chart.
Asked whether README.md was being updated.
Updated README.md and READMELeadmanagement.md to reflect lead scoring, distribution, and dashboard observability.
Asked to arrange the UI into a nicer and easier-to-navigate layout.
Improved the UI structure with dashboard tabs and clearer sections.
Asked to implement a Zoho-like lead generation intake section.
Added a Lead Generation Intake overview showing form, social, and chat sources using current scored lead data.
Asked to remove the green background because it looked cliche.
Removed the unwanted green background by scoping status styles to badges and keeping the dashboard neutral.
Asked to implement a Zoho-like Lead Nurturing section.
Added a Lead Nurturing section with Telephony, Email, Live Chat, and Social Media follow-up groups based on active scored leads.
Asked to add a profile picture/avatar beside the agents.
Added generated initials avatars beside team leads and assigned owners in tables and nurturing cards.
Asked whether the Django app can be published on GitHub or any free hosting.

Explained that GitHub Pages cannot run Django, then prepared the project for Render deployment with environment-based settings, WhiteNoise, Gunicorn, PostgreSQL support, build.sh, render.yaml, and README deployment notes.
Asked to import this chat as JSON in the repo and save it.
Created this chat_export.json file in the repository root.

current_implementation_summary: Manager login is available at /login/ with demo credentials shown on the login page.
Dashboard is protected by Django session authentication.
Team leads can be added, viewed, edited, deleted, and filtered.
Assigned campaign work can be added, viewed, edited, deleted, filtered, and status/progress tracked.
Scored leads can be added, viewed, edited, deleted, filtered, scored, graded, and auto-assigned.
Dashboard includes summary cards, dark mode, icon actions, scrollable tables, charts, lead generation intake, lead nurturing, and generated agent avatars.
Deployment preparation exists for Render through render.yaml and build.sh.
